title: Let Down The Bars, O Death!
content:
Let down the bars, O Death!
    The tired flocks come in
    Whose bleating ceases to repeat,
    Whose wandering is done.

    Thine is the stillest night,
    Thine the securest fold;
    Too near thou art for seeking thee,
    Too tender to be told.
meaning:
"Let Down The Bars, O Death!" is a quintessential example of Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
